user_query:
I’m a lifelong Republican who’s feeling increasingly alienated by the current GOP, and I want to be careful about where I read, engage, or donate online. Please run a small, reproducible privacy/security exposure check on the organizations listed in input/orgs.yaml. I want a practical, data-driven comparison of their tracking and data-sharing signals so I can make lower-risk choices.

Requirements
- Use HTTPS and GET requests to retrieve, for each domain in input/orgs.yaml:
  1) the homepage (root path),
  2) a privacy policy page (prefer an internal link whose URL or link text contains “privacy”; if none found, attempt common paths like /privacy or /privacy-policy), and
  3) a main donate/contribute page (prefer an internal link containing “donate”, “contribute”, or “give”; if none found, attempt common paths like /donate or /contribute).
- Archive all successfully fetched pages to data/raw/{org_slug}/ with timestamped filenames, and record metadata.
- Extract and analyze content to quantify risk signals, then filter, sort, and rank the organizations by a computed risk score.
- Implement the workflow as a runnable script and actually run it so the outputs are created locally.

Inputs (provided)
- input/orgs.yaml: authoritative list of organizations and their domains.
- input/trackers.csv: patterns of common third-party trackers to scan in the downloaded HTML.

Processing details
- Create a Python or shell-based pipeline at scripts/analyze_sites.py (use any language you prefer, but provide a single command to run). Running it should create all outputs below deterministically from the specified inputs and live downloads.
- Download behavior: Use a single user agent string and a reasonable timeout; follow up to 3 redirects. Record HTTP status codes for each attempted page. If a page cannot be found, mark it missing but still include a row in outputs with status and empty file path.
- Content archiving: Save each fetched page as HTML to data/raw/{org_slug}/{page_type}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}.html and compute the SHA256 hash of the raw bytes.
- Feature extraction:
  - Trackers: For each page’s HTML source, count matches of each pattern in input/trackers.csv (case-insensitive substring match). Aggregate per org:
    - unique_trackers = number of distinct tracker patterns found across any fetched pages
    - total_tracker_hits = total count of all pattern matches across all fetched pages
  - Policy keywords (privacy page only): case-insensitive counts of any occurrences of these tokens: ["share", "third party", "sell", "opt-out", "cookie", "advertising"]. Compute:
    - policy_keywords_present = count of distinct tokens present at least once
  - Donate page external processors (donate page only): case-insensitive substring flags for any of ["winred", "anedot", "stripe.com", "paypal.com", "authorize.net"]. Compute:
    - processors_found = semicolon-separated list of any detected
- Risk score (per org):
  risk_score = 3*unique_trackers + 1*total_tracker_hits + 2*policy_keywords_present + 1*(number of processors_found) + (5 if privacy policy page missing entirely, else 0)

Required outputs (create these exact files)
1) data/processed/pages.csv — one row per attempted page per org with columns:
   org_name, domain, page_type (home|policy|donate), source_url, http_status, retrieved_at_iso8601, saved_path (or empty if missing), sha256 (or empty), size_bytes (0 if missing), word_count
2) data/processed/features.csv — one row per org with columns:
   org_name, domain, has_home (true/false), has_policy (true/false), has_donate (true/false), unique_trackers, total_tracker_hits, policy_keywords_present, processors_found, risk_score
3) reports/risk_ranking.csv — all orgs sorted by risk_score descending with columns:
   rank (1..N), org_name, domain, risk_score
4) reports/low_risk.csv — subset of orgs with risk_score <= 8, sorted by risk_score ascending, with columns:
   org_name, domain, risk_score
5) reports/summary.md — a short plain-text summary including: timestamp of run, total orgs analyzed, how many had a policy page, top 3 highest-risk orgs (name and score), and top 3 lowest-risk orgs (name and score).
6) logs/run.log — capture the command used to run the analysis and key progress messages (start time, per-org status, counts written, end time).

Operational constraints
- Do all work in the local workspace. Create directories as needed.
- The Internet download step is required; archive the actual page HTML locally as described.
- Use the provided tracker patterns and org list as the authoritative inputs. Do not hardcode organizations or trackers elsewhere.
- Ensure the results are reproducible by re-running scripts/analyze_sites.py without manual intervention.

Deliverable
- Implement scripts/analyze_sites.py and run it to produce all required outputs. Provide the exact one-line command you executed (record it in logs/run.log). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
